fr.recommendations: Ensuring that the single access point is clearly marked and kept free of obstructions may enhance emergency responder access. To facilitate efficient movement and staging, maintaining ample space around the main building and tennis court for vehicle maneuvering could prove beneficial. Additionally, utilizing the open terrain and open green space appropriately may support navigation and staging in emergency situations. Regular maintenance of pedestrian walkways to keep them clear would assist responders in moving quickly and safely around the property. Furthermore, ensuring the landscaped areas do not impede visibility or access routes could enhance responder efficiency and effectiveness during an emergency operation.

wildfire.recommendations: Improving wildfire resilience and property preparedness could be achieved by enhancing defensible space and reducing fuel continuity around the property. Managing and reducing vegetation adjacent to the structure, such as trimming or thinning shrubs and trees, would help mitigate potential fire exposure. Expanding open areas and maintaining the existing green space and surrounding open space can help act as natural fuel breaks, thereby limiting fire spread opportunities. Consider reducing brush concentrations, particularly near access routes, to enhance firefighter access and support suppression efforts. Additionally, maintaining vegetation separation and managing any vegetated perimeters can further support long-term wildfire preparedness and mitigate potential fire pathways toward the structure.
